lede: A look at November 15 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including TyTy Washington Jr. and Sania Mirza, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: World population
events.desc: The world population reached eight billion.

events.title: Xi Jinping
events.desc: Xi Jinping becomes General Secretary of the Chinese Communist Party and a new seven-member Politburo Standing Committee is inaugurated.

events.title: People's Republic of Bulgaria
events.desc: The Communist People's Republic of Bulgaria is disestablished and a new republican government is instituted.

events.title: Vietnam War
events.desc: Vietnam War: In Washington, D.C., 250,000–500,000 protesters staged a peaceful demonstration against the war, including a symbolic "March Against Death".

events.title: Clutter family murders
events.desc: Four members of the Clutter family are murdered near Holcomb, Kansas, by Perry Smith and Richard Hickock, a crime later detailed by Truman Capote in his 1966 non-fiction novel In Cold Blood.

events.title: The Holocaust
events.desc: The Holocaust: German SS leader Heinrich Himmler orders that Gypsies are to be put "on the same level as Jews and placed in concentration camps".

events.title: 1922 Guayaquil general strike
events.desc: At least 300 are massacred during a general strike in Guayaquil, Ecuador.

events.title: Free City of Danzig
events.desc: The Free City of Danzig is established.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: Union General William Tecumseh Sherman begins his March to the Sea through Georgia towards the city of Savannah.

events.title: Castellania (Valletta)
events.desc: The secondly-built Castellania in Valletta is officially inaugurated with the blessing of the interior Chapel of Sorrows.

deaths.text: David K. Wyatt, American historian and author
Edoardo Agnelli, son of industrialist Gianni Agnelli
Alfred Kennerley, English-Australian politician, 10th Premier of Tasmania

observances.text: Christian feast day: Blessed Caius of Korea
Christian feast day: Francis Asbury and George Whitefield (Episcopal Church)
Christian feast day: Leopold III, a public holiday in Lower Austria and Vienna.
